title: BACnet Object Access Audit
app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884)
spl:
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:bacnet:json"
| where match(pdu_service,"(?i)write.*property") OR pdu_service="Write_Property_Request"
| where object_type IN ("analog-output","binary-output","schedule","life-safety-point") OR match(property,"(?i)present.value|priority")
| stats count by source_h destination_h object_type instance_number property
| sort - count
description: Writes to analog outputs, schedules, or life-safety objects can change building or process environmental limits; auditing ReadProperty/WriteProperty supports both cyber and operational accountability.
value: Writes to analog outputs, schedules, or life-safety objects can change building or process environmental limits; auditing ReadProperty/WriteProperty supports both cyber and operational accountability.
implementation: Deploy ICSNPP-BACnet on UDP/47808 taps; use `bacnet_property` fields when the TA routes them into the same sourcetype or a dedicated property sourcetype. Define sensitive object lists per site; alert on writes from non-BMS servers.
visualization: Table (writes by object), Bar chart (writes by source_h), Timeline (write bursts).
knownFalsePositives: Object read/write activity during HVAC schedules, trend uploads, or commissioning of actuators.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:bacnet:json"` (`bacnet_property.log` via `pdu_service`, `object_type`, `property`, `value`, `source_h`, `destination_h`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Deploy ICSNPP-BACnet on UDP/47808 taps; use `bacnet_property` fields when the TA routes them into the same sourcetype or a dedicated property sourcetype. Define sensitive object lists per site; alert on writes from non-BMS servers.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot sourcetype="zeek:bacnet:json"
| where match(pdu_service,"(?i)write.*property") OR pdu_service="Write_Property_Request"
| where object_type IN ("analog-output","binary-output","schedule","life-safety-point") OR match(property,"(?i)present.value|priority")
| stats count by source_h destination_h object_type instance_number property
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**BACnet Object Access Audit** ‚Äî Writes to analog outputs, schedules, or life-safety objects can change building or process environmental limits; auditing ReadProperty/WriteProperty supports both cyber and operational accountability.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot` `sourcetype="zeek:bacnet:json"` (`bacnet_property.log` via `pdu_service`, `object_type`, `property`, `value`, `source_h`, `destination_h`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TA for Zeek (Splunkbase 5466), Corelight App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3884). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot; **sourcetype**: zeek:bacnet:json. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ot, sourcetype="zeek:bacnet:json". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(pdu_service,"(?i)write.*property") OR pdu_service="Write_Property_Request"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where object_type IN ("analog-output","binary-output","schedule","life-safety-point") OR match(property,"(?i)present.value|‚Ä¶` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by source_h destination_h object_type instance_number property** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Run the search for a short window and compare key fields with Zeek or Corelight protocol logs on the sensor for the same flow. Reconcile function codes, endpoints, and time order against a small known-good capture if needed.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (writes by object), Bar chart (writes by source_h), Timeline (write bursts).
grandmaExplanation: We use deep packet inspection to audit BACnet object access so we know who is reading and writing what in the building or plant.
